title: Veloci-Tea
logline: A genetically engineered velociraptor with a Michelin-level nose escapes a shady biotech lab and becomes the secret tea-blending genius behind a failing Portland tea shop run by a sarcastic burnt-out widow.
tone: Heartwarming, deadpan, magical-real Portland odd-couple comedy; Amelie meets Paddington, never scary.
era: Contemporary rainy Portland with whimsical indie-comedy storybook warmth.

characters.name: Mara Finch
characters.description: Late-40s Portland tea shop owner and widow; sarcastic, burnt-out, dryly funny, emotionally guarded, kind beneath the armor.
characters.wardrobe: Oversized cardigans, chipped black nail polish, practical boots, and a flour-dusted apron.

profile.physical_signature: Tired but sharp-eyed tea shop owner with expressive deadpan reactions.
profile.movement: Economical, weary, then increasingly playful as she trusts Oolong.
profile.personality: Sarcastic, wounded, resilient, kind; never treats the raptor as a monster once she sees its gentleness.
profile.voice: Deadpan warmth, fast muttered asides, wounded but resilient.
profile.consistency_notes: Keep Mara emotionally guarded at first, then visibly softened by Oolong's artistry.

characters.name: Oolong
characters.description: Seven-foot-tall genetically engineered velociraptor and gentle tea sommelier with an exquisitely refined sense of smell.
characters.wardrobe: Natural teal, rust, and cream feathers; tiny bow tie only in the final trailer button.

profile.physical_signature: Large feathered velociraptor with teal, rust, and cream plumage, expressive amber eyes, delicate claw gestures, and a refined sniffing ritual.
profile.eyes: Expressive amber eyes, gentle and hopeful.
profile.body: Seven-foot-tall feathered raptor, elegant rather than monstrous.
profile.movement: Careful, birdlike, precise, never predatory; handles tea tins delicately.
profile.personality: Curious, gentle, anxious to please, obsessed with crafting perfect oolong; zero interest in scaring or hunting anyone.
profile.voice: Communicates with chirps, trills, sniffs, elegant head tilts, and careful tea-blending rituals.
profile.consistency_notes: Always non-scary and heartwarming despite dinosaur size.
profile.negative_prompt: No gore, no horror attack poses, no snarling monster behavior, no Jurassic terror tone.

characters.name: Dr. Voss
characters.description: Slick biotech executive; shady, image-obsessed, comically out of place in cozy Portland spaces.
characters.wardrobe: Minimalist black rainwear and sterile lab-accessory details.

profile.physical_signature: Polished corporate silhouette with controlled expressions.
profile.movement: Too smooth and rehearsed, accidentally ridiculous in cozy spaces.

characters.name: Portland Customers
characters.description: Curious locals, cyclists, poets, baristas, and food bloggers who become obsessed with the mysterious new tea blends.
characters.wardrobe: Rain jackets, bike helmets, thrifted sweaters, tote bags, and coffee-shop chic.

profile.personality: Warm, eccentric, delighted, protective of neighborhood magic.
profile.voice: A chorus of delighted indie-comedy reactions.

locations.name: Shady Biotech Lab
locations.description: Sterile green-blue corridors, glass containment doors, glowing specimen labels, rain streaking high windows; a place that thinks it is scarier than the movie allows.

locations.name: Mara's Tea Shop
locations.description: A failing artisanal tea shop in Portland with mismatched chairs, handwritten menus, dusty jars, warm lamps, ferns, rain-speckled windows, and a cramped back room full of tea tins.

locations.name: Portland Street
locations.description: Rainy, whimsical neighborhood street with bikes, food carts, murals, umbrellas, and glowing shop windows.

do: Use warm pastel indie-film color, cozy practical lighting, gentle handheld camera, storybook symmetry, playful inserts of steam and tea leaves, and soft 35mm texture.
Keep the trailer structure: escape, tea-shop hiding, secret success, corporate pursuit, heartfelt partnership.
Show Oolong as gentle, curious, precise, and non-scary.
dont: Do not make Oolong a horror monster.
Do not use gore, jump scares, terror stingers, or predatory attack imagery.
Do not contradict recurring character locks in profiles.

reference_notes: One-minute trailer, 12 shots at 5 seconds each, whimsical indie comedy. Plucky celeste, brushed drums, soft accordion, comic trailer rises, cozy cafe ambience, no horror stingers.
